qids.subject: Theory of Computation
qids.prose: L e t be a regular language, be a deterministic context-free language and a recursively enumerable, but not recursive, language. Which one of the following statements is false? A. is a deterministic CFL B. is recursive C. is context free D. is recursively enumerable

qids.subject: Theory of Computation
qids.prose: The language over the alphabet is: A. not recursive B. is recursive and is a deterministic CFL C. is a regular language D. is not a deterministic CFL but a CFL

qids.subject: Theory of Computation
qids.prose: Match the following: A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Theory of Computation
qids.prose: Which of the following is true for the language A. It is not accepted by a Turing Machine B. It is regular but not context-free C. It is context-free but not regular D. It is neither regular nor context-free, but accepted by a Turing machine
